project.name: Ryewired

status.summary: Ryewired is fully architected but still under active development. The immediate priority is resolving existing bugs and refining the current systems before implementing the next major evolution of the application.
priorities: Transition toward the integrated workbench model.

project.description: Ryewired is a browser-based virtual breadboard for designing, prototyping, experimenting with, and learning analog audio circuits, particularly guitar effects pedals. It is intended to replicate the experience of building pedals on a physical solderless breadboard while taking advantage of capabilities unique to software.

source_of_truth.implementation: The accompanying project ZIP is authoritative.
source_of_truth.purpose: This document exists to provide architectural intent, product philosophy, design goals, and future direction that cannot be inferred directly from the source code.
source_of_truth.rule: If this document contradicts the implementation, assume the implementation is correct unless the user explicitly states otherwise.

project_philosophy.primary_goal: Create the software equivalent of sitting at a real guitar pedal prototyping bench.
project_philosophy.secondary_goal: Allow users to move from an idea to hearing that idea as quickly and naturally as possible.
principles: The software should behave like a real electronics workbench.
The user should build circuits, not infrastructure.
Visual realism is preferred over abstract symbolism.
Behavioral realism is preferred over mathematically exhaustive simulation.
Learning and experimentation are equally important.
Favor elegant, maintainable architecture over clever implementations.

design_principles: Components should resemble their physical through-hole counterparts.
The breadboard should remain the visual center of the application.
The interface should stay approachable for beginners while remaining useful to experienced builders.
Animations should be subtle and purposeful.
New features should integrate naturally into existing workflows.
Shared functionality should exist in one authoritative implementation.
Avoid duplicate rendering or business logic.
engineering_principles: Read and understand the existing architecture before introducing new systems.
Prefer incremental improvements over sweeping refactors.
Maintain backwards compatibility with saved layouts whenever possible.
Keep modules focused on a single responsibility.

simulation_philosophy.goal: Produce intuitive, educational, and musically useful results rather than mathematically perfect electrical analysis.

current_state.summary: The current architecture is considered solid. Existing work should focus primarily on correctness, stability, and polish before major feature expansion.

future_direction.vision: Transform Ryewired from a virtual breadboard into a complete virtual pedal development workstation.

future_workbench.description: The workbench should provide the permanent infrastructure required for prototyping. Users should only build the pedal circuit itself.

permanent_devices.name: Power Supply
permanent_devices.description: Permanent adjustable DC power source connected directly to the breadboard power rails.

permanent_devices.name: True Bypass Footswitch
permanent_devices.description: Permanent bypass switch controlling whether audio travels through the circuit or bypasses it.

expected_workflow: Power supply, Input, Output, True Bypass switch, and Status LED already exist.
Hear the clean input signal immediately.
Observe the Oscilloscope and FFT displaying the signal.
Construct a pedal circuit between Input and Output.
Toggle the bypass switch to compare clean and processed audio instantly.
ux_goals: A new project should produce audible output within seconds.
Users should spend their time designing circuits instead of configuring infrastructure.
The workbench should feel immediately familiar to anyone who has prototyped a pedal on a real breadboard.
Every interaction should reinforce understanding rather than introduce unnecessary complexity.
assistant_guidelines: Read the source code before proposing architectural changes.
Assume existing design decisions were intentional.
Prefer extending existing systems over replacing them.
Do not rewrite working systems without compelling justification.
Maintain consistency with the established code style.
When uncertain, ask before making significant architectural decisions.

north_star.vision: Ryewired should become the software equivalent of a guitarist's physical prototyping bench.
north_star.mission: Reduce the friction between an idea and hearing that idea through a virtual circuit.
core_principles: The workbench provides the infrastructure. The user builds only the circuit.
Real-world workflows take precedence over abstract electronic concepts.
Visual authenticity should reinforce understanding rather than decorate the interface.
Behavior should be predictable, educational, and musically useful.
Every feature should shorten the path from experimentation to audible results.
success_metrics: A new project produces audio immediately.
Users focus on designing circuits instead of configuring the environment.
Switching between bypass and effect is instantaneous.
The software feels like using a real pedal prototyping bench.
The application remains approachable for beginners while deep enough for experienced builders.
north_star.long_term_goal: Ryewired should feel less like a circuit simulator and more like a dedicated pedal development workstation with integrated power, signal routing, bypass switching, analysis tools, and an intuitive breadboard workspace.
